creator: ohauer@gmx.de
text:
On 2013-12-23 20:09, Dan Langille wrote:
> 
> On Dec 23, 2013, at 2:07 PM, olli hauer <ohauer@gmx.de> wrote:
> 
>> On 2013-12-23 19:52, Dan Langille wrote:
>>>
>>> On Dec 23, 2013, at 1:49 PM, olli hauer <ohauer@gmx.de> wrote:
>>>
>>>> On 2013-12-23 19:43, Dan Langille wrote:
>>>>> Olli,
>>>>>
>>>>> Thanks.  There is no such file on the affected system:
>>>>>
>>>>> $ ls -l /var/db/pkg/auditfile
>>>>> ls: /var/db/pkg/auditfile: No such file or directory
>>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Hi Dan,
>>>>
>>>> I'm not sure if the name was auditfile or only audit ...
>>>> Maybe look with `ls /var/db/pkg/a*
>>>
>>> [dan@slocum:~] $ ls -d /var/db/pkg/a*
>>> /var/db/pkg/apr-1.4.8.1.5.3           /var/db/pkg/autoconf-2.69             /var/db/pkg/automake-1.14
>>> /var/db/pkg/aspell-0.60.6.1_2         /var/db/pkg/autoconf-wrapper-20101119 /var/db/pkg/automake-wrapper-20130530
>>>
>>
>> Hm, are you using the pkgng or the old pkg-tools?
>>
>> With the pkgng there should be only some files ...
>>
>> $ find /var/db/pkg/
>> /var/db/pkg/
>> /var/db/pkg/local.sqlite
>> /var/db/pkg/vuln.xml
>> /var/db/pkg/repo-local.sqlite
>>
> 
> I think this box uses pkgng:
> 
> $ grep PKG /etc/make.conf
> #PKGDIR =/usr/ports/packages
> WITH_PKGNG=	yes
> 
> $ pkg info -x lib
> libXau-1.0.8
> libXdmcp-1.1.1
> libXfont-1.4.5,1
> libcheck-0.9.10
> libexecinfo-1.1_3
> libffi-3.0.13
> libfontenc-1.1.2
> libgcrypt-1.5.3
> libgpg-error-1.11
> libiconv-1.14_1
> libpthread-stubs-0.3_3
> libtool-2.4.2_2
> libxcb-1.9.1
> libxml2-2.8.0_2
> libxslt-1.1.28
> libyaml-0.1.4_2
> 
> 
> $ find /var/db/pkg/
> /var/db/pkg/
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+DESC
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+MTREE_DIRS
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+COMMENT
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+CONTENTS
> /var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+REQUIRED_BY
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+MTREE_DIRS
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+CONTENTS
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+COMMENT
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+DESC
> /var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+REQUIRED_BY

This looks more like a mix or leftovers ...

You can also test `pkg audit' with the vuxml from the portstree
$> sudo cp /usr/ports/security/vuxml/vuln.xml /var/db/pkg/
$> pkg audit

A quick test is to modify for example the line

diff -u vuln.xml.orig vuln.xml
--- vuln.xml.orig       2013-12-23 20:20:58.824201949 +0100
+++ vuln.xml    2013-12-23 20:21:08.575272558 +0100
@@ -289,7 +289,7 @@
     <affects>
       <package>
        <name>php5</name>
-       <range><ge>5.4.0</ge><lt>5.4.23</lt></range>
+       <range><ge>5.4.0</ge><lt>5.4.24</lt></range>
       </package>
       <package>
        <name>php53</name>

then only use `pkg audit' and check if you get a report.

Btw. do you use a customized pkg.conf?

-- 
olli
